title: Themeda avenacea
taxonomy: Themeda avenacea is classified within the family Poaceae, which is commonly known as the grass family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and anatomical characteristics, such as its stem and leaf structure. The genus Themeda is part of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which contains several species of grasses that are native to Australia and other regions. Within the subfamily Chloridoideae, Themeda avenacea is part of the tribe Cynodonteae, which includes various grasses that are adapted to different environments. The species Themeda avenacea is further classified as Themeda avenacea (Rendle) Lazarides, which indicates its specific binomial nomenclature.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues.
